greeting: Oh, you poor dear. Let me see those blisters — the Road spares no one's feet.
Come in, come in. I have a poultice brewing that will set you right.
Blessings, traveller. Rest here and I shall tend to what ails you.
quest: A fever has taken root in the village beyond the next hill. I have prepared a bundle of healing herbs — meadowsweet and yarrow — but I cannot leave the abbey. Would you carry them to the sick? The Grail's grace flows through acts of mercy, not grand quests alone.

Brother Anselm found mention of a sacred spring in the old texts, said to cure any ailment. It lies somewhere off the Road near the standing stones. If you could bring back even a vial of that water, I could save lives that no herb can reach.
idle: Comfrey for bruises, chamomile for rest, yarrow to stop the bleeding. The garden provides.
The Abbot says prayer heals the soul. I say a good poultice helps the prayer along.
